prose-li:mb-2 prose-ul:mb-6 prose-ul:mt-4 prose-h3:mt-10 prose-h3:mb-4"><section id="conversion-fundamentals" class="mb-16 scroll-mt-24"><h2 class="font-display text-2xl md:text-3xl mb-8">Property Landing Page Fundamentals</h2><div class="text-muted-foreground leading-[1.8] space-y-5"><p>Before diving into design tactics, understand what property buyers actually need to convert:</p><ul><li><strong>Trust signals</strong> — They're considering a major financial decision</li><li><strong>Visual confidence</strong> — They need to visualise themselves there</li><li><strong>Information accessibility</strong> — Key details without hunting</li><li><strong>Low-friction contact</strong> — Multiple easy ways to get in touch</li><li><strong>Social proof</strong> — Evidence that others trust you</li></ul><p>Every element on your page should serve one of these needs. Anything else is distraction.</p></div></section><section id="above-the-fold" class="mb-16 scroll-mt-24"><h2 class="font-display text-2xl md:text-3xl mb-8">Above the Fold: The Critical First Impression</h2><div class="text-muted-foreground leading-[1.8] space-y-5"><p>You have 3-5 seconds to convince visitors to stay. Above the fold content must immediately communicate value.</p><h3>Essential Elements</h3><ul><li><strong>Hero image</strong> — High-quality, emotionally resonant property photography</li><li><strong>Clear headline</strong> — Development name + location + key differentiator</li><li><strong>Price indicator</strong> — Starting from £X (don't hide pricing)</li><li><strong>Primary CTA</strong> — "Book a Viewing" or "Download Brochure"</li><li><strong>Trust badge</strong> — Award, certification, or developer logo</li></ul><h3>What to Avoid</h3><ul><li>Autoplay videos that slow page load</li><li>Vague taglines without specific value</li><li>Navigation-heavy headers that distract from the CTA</li><li>Stock imagery that looks generic</li></ul></div></section><section id="form-optimisation" class="mb-16 scroll-mt-24"><h2 class="font-display text-2xl md:text-3xl mb-8">Form Optimisation for Property Enquiries</h2><div class="text-muted-foreground leading-[1.8] space-y-5"><p>Your form is where conversions happen—or don't. Every additional field reduces completion rates.</p><h3>Optimal Form Structure</h3><ul><li><strong>Essential fields only</strong> — Name, email, phone (that's it for initial enquiry)</li><li><strong>Progressive profiling</strong> — Collect additional info after initial contact</li><li><strong>Smart defaults</strong> — Pre-select the most common options</li><li><strong>Clear privacy messaging</strong> — GDPR compliance without friction</li></ul><h3>Form Placement</h3><ul><li>Above the fold for landing pages</li><li>Sticky sidebar on desktop for development pages</li><li>Fixed bottom bar on mobile</li><li>Exit-intent popup (used sparingly)</li></ul><h3>Button Psychology</h3><p>"Submit" is the worst CTA. Use action-oriented, benefit-focused text:</p><ul><li>"Book My Viewing"</li><li>"Get Floor Plans"</li><li>"Request Callback"</li><li>"Check Availability"</li></ul></div></section><section id="mobile-experience" class="mb-16 scroll-mt-24"><h2 class="font-display text-2xl md:text-3xl mb-8">Mobile-First Property Pages</h2><div class="text-muted-foreground leading-[1.8] space-y-5"><p>60%+ of property searches happen on mobile. Yet most property websites are designed desktop-first and then squeezed onto smaller screens.</p><h3>Mobile Priorities</h3><ul><li><strong>Tap targets</strong> — Buttons minimum 44px height, with adequate spacing</li><li><strong>Click-to-call</strong> — Phone numbers that dial when tapped</li><li><strong>Image galleries</strong> — Swipe-friendly, fast-loading, full-screen option</li><li><strong>Sticky CTA</strong> — Always-visible contact button at bottom</li><li><strong>Accordion content</strong> — Expandable sections to reduce scroll</li></ul><h3>Page Speed on Mobile</h3><p>Property pages are image-heavy. Optimise ruthlessly:</p><ul><li>WebP format for all images</li><li>Lazy loading below the fold</li><li>Responsive image sizes (don't serve desktop images to mobile)</li><li>Target: under 3 seconds to interactive</li></ul></div></section><section id="trust-and-urgency" class="mb-16 scroll-mt-24"><h2 class="font-display text-2xl md:text-3xl mb-8">Building Trust and Creating Urgency</h2><div class="text-muted-foreground leading-[1.8] space-y-5"><p>Property purchases are high-stakes decisions. Your page must address the fear of making the wrong choice while motivating action.</p><h3>Trust Elements</h3><ul><li><strong>Testimonials</strong> — Specific quotes from recent buyers/tenants</li><li><strong>Developer track record</strong> — Previous projects, completions, awards</li><li><strong>Agent credentials</strong> — Professional memberships, local expertise</li><li><strong>Press coverage</strong> — Media logos and quotes</li><li><strong>Live availability</strong> — Real-time status reduces "is this still available?" friction</li></ul><h3>Urgency Tactics (Use Ethically)</h3><ul><li><strong>Genuine scarcity</strong> — "3 units remaining in Phase 1"</li><li><strong>Launch incentives</strong> — "Stamp duty paid for reservations before [date]"</li><li><strong>Price increases</strong> — "Phase 2 prices from [date]"</li><li><strong>Interest indicators</strong> — "12 viewings booked this week"</li></ul><p>Never fabricate urgency. Buyers see through fake scarcity, and it destroys trust.</p></div></section><section id="testing-and-iteration" class="mb-16 scroll-mt-24"><h2 class="font-display text-2xl md:text-3xl mb-8">Testing and Continuous Improvement</h2><div class="text-muted-foreground leading-[1.8] space-y-5"><p>The best property landing pages are built through iteration, not guesswork. Set up proper measurement and test systematically.</p><h3>Key Metrics to Track</h3><ul><li><strong>Conversion rate</strong> — Enquiries ÷ unique visitors</li><li><strong>Form abandonment</strong> — Started vs completed forms</li><li><strong>Scroll depth</strong> — How far do visitors read?</li><li><strong>Time to conversion</strong> — How long before they enquire?</li><li><strong>Device split</strong> — Mobile vs desktop conversion rates</li></ul><h3>A/B Testing Priorities</h3><p>Test in order of impact:</p><ul><li>Headlines and value propositions</li><li>CTA button text and placement</li><li>Form length and fields</li><li>Hero image selection</li><li>Social proof positioning</li></ul></div></section><section class="mb-12 p-6 bg-primary/5 rounded-xl border border-primary/20"><h2 class="font-display text-2xl mb-4">How we do this at <span aria-label="iNDEXHILL"><span class="font-bold">iNDEXHILL</span></span></h2><div class="text-muted-foreground
